text: Highlight Clip Crypto Penetration Is Still Below 1% in Terms of Global Wealth Crypto Penetration Is Still Below 1% in Terms of Global Wealth Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said in a July 16 interview with Talking Tokens Podcast that while many people believe the crypto market is already saturated, crypto penetration remains below 1% in terms of global wealth, meaning the industry is still at an early stage. He said fiat on- and off-ramps still involve significant friction, while stablecoins have yet to offer both attractive yields and easy tradability. In the RWA sector, only a small number of stocks have been tokenized, mostly in the U.S., leaving substantial room for further growth. — link

text: Movement Labs Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy With Liabilities Above $1 Million Movement Labs, the developer of the Movement blockchain, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. Court filings show the company has between $100,000 and $500,000 in assets, more than $1 million in liabilities and fewer than 1,000 creditors. Its largest creditors include co-founder Rushi Manche, the Delaware Division of Revenue and Anchorage Digital. The filing follows controversy over MOVE token market-making, an internal investigation and Binance's ban of the related market-making account. Movement Labs parted ways with Manche in May 2025. — link

text: Mid week sidequest. As I mentioned, I had the opportunity last week to help a friend who was early on building some cool tech on RH. For the past two years I've essentially been trading on the sidelines and writing content on narratives, tech and memes which I've found interesting or bought. Before that I was the CMO for one of the biggest launches of 2024 that did a 50x from public sale which inevitably blew up for reasons outside of my control. While I enjoy trading and I don't think there's a profession that can beat it, I do still get the itch to build from time to time as scaling a company, product and marketing has its own meta games. From the outside it looks easy and I see many successful traders pivoting to building a company, in hopes to find more purpose which it can definitely bring, but at the same time its also incredibly frustrating and often feels like you're playing firefighter simulator and managing small crucial tasks all day long. Since it's been such a long time I thought I'd try my hand and see how much I could get done in a short time window for Prizefight. The dev is a cracked coder and had a unique angle for his product except he didn't have much experience with the positioning, content, branding etc. In 72 hours we were able to do a full rebrand of the website and product which you can see in the photos, a cinematic animation and content pipeline from scratch. Virtuals liked our work: Anyone who's worked in marketing and product would know how hard it is to deliver something like this in such a short time window even if you had a team fully ready to get started… we didn't. Im a perfectionist but we had to cut corners to get an initial concept out. Attention to detail is what will make or break your success as a builder. When working on tight deadlines in a situation like this, your objective is to get 80% of the way there and then running at full speed to deploy live while improving small features post deployment. This isn't a final version and still some important changes to be made, thought I'd give some insight to those curious. Even though marketing has been my bread and butter since I first entered crypto, I had doubts since I hadn't gotten my hands dirty in quite a while. Glad to know I still got a bit of magic. Made me realize I could easily start my own company or become CEO/CMO again if I ever wished and still have the tenacity for extreme output in short time frames. Was hesitant to even share this but I was quite proud of what we achieved in such a short time frame. Most people limit themselves to what they think can be a viable way of bag working or supporting friends. While it's a niche ability and skill to have a holistic understanding of marketing, branding and positioning, it's one of the most crucial aspects that create people's perception of your token and product.

text: Казахстан делает еще один важный шаг на пути к становлению глобального центра технологических инноваций. Министерство цифрового развития, инноваций и аэрокосмической промышленности Республики Казахстан и Network School подписали Меморандум о сотрудничестве в сфере искусственного интеллекта, инноваций, технологического предпринимательства и развития человеческого капитала. Network School, основанная Баладжи Шринивасаном — бывшим CTO Coinbase, экс-партнером Andreessen Horowitz и одним из самых известных технологических инвесторов Кремниевой долины, - развивает новую модель образования для эпохи ИИ. Одним из ключевых направлений станет создание первого кампуса Network School в Казахстане. Также партнерство включает развитие образовательных программ по ИИ, поддержку стартапов, проведение совместных исследований, международных конференций и привлечение в страну ведущих экспертов, технологических компаний и инвестиций. Мы рады видеть такие инициативы в Казахстане и продолжим поддерживать развитие технологической экосистемы, объединяя предпринимателей, международных партнеров и новые возможности для роста.

text: South Korea's Top Five Crypto Exchanges See Volume Plunge 88% as Korbit Sells 15 BTC and 60 ETH to Fund Operations According to ZDNet Korea, daily trading volume across South Korea's five major won-based crypto exchanges fell 88% year-on-year to about KRW 412.7 billion ($280 million) as of July 20. The downturn has hit fee revenue, with Upbit operator Dunamu reporting first-quarter revenue and operating profit declines of 55% and 78%, respectively. Korbit has sold crypto assets three times this year to fund operations, including 15 BTC and 60 ETH in early July for about KRW 1.6 billion ($1.08 million). — link

text: BlockSec: Wanchain Cardano Bridge Reportedly Exploited, 515M NIGHT Drained BlockSec's Phalcon said Wanchain's Cardano bridge was reportedly exploited, with approximately 515 million NIGHT drained from the bridge treasury. Initial analysis suggests the issue may stem from non-injective signed-message encoding in the TreasuryCheck validator, potentially enabling signature reuse attacks. NIGHT is the native token of Midnight, a privacy-focused blockchain project incubated by Input Output (IO), the company behind Cardano. Following the incident, NIGHT fell more than 30% intraday to around $0.016, hitting a record low. Based on current prices, the drained tokens are worth roughly $9 million. — link

text: Base Head: Working With Coinbase on 1:1 Asset-Backed Tokenized Stocks Base head Jesse Pollak said in a post on X that Robinhood Chain's launch of tokenized stocks in an EVM environment is the right direction. While Base is behind in this area, it is working with Coinbase on a related product. Pollak said Base plans to offer tokenized stocks backed 1:1 by underlying shares, rather than using Robinhood's derivatives model. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip Julie Biel: SpaceX Falls for a Sixth Straight Day, Down 45% From Peak Julie Biel: SpaceX Falls for a Sixth Straight Day, Down 45% From Peak Julie Biel, Chief Market Strategist at Kayne Anderson Rudnick, said on CNBC's Fast Money that SpaceX still should not be considered cheap even if it falls back near its roughly $135 IPO price. She argued that much of the company's original valuation logic was driven by xAI rather than just rockets, Starlink, and Starship, and that a pre-merger sum-of-the-parts value for SpaceX and xAI may have been closer to $900 billion. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Strategy Says Bitcoin Reserve Could Cover 31 Years of Dividends Strategy Corporate Treasurer Chaitanya Jain said the company's Bitcoin reserve could cover approximately 31 years of dividends, while its U.S. dollar reserve could cover around 1.8 years. Strategy founder Michael Saylor said that, as of July 19, the company held 843,775 BTC and had increased its dollar reserve by $225 million to $3.2 billion. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Spot Bitcoin ETFs Record $227M in Net Inflows on July 20 On July 20 (ET), U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded total net inflows of $227 million, marking the fifth consecutive day of net inflows. U.S. spot Ethereum ETFs saw total net inflows of $38.09 million. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: ENS DAO Approves Two-Year Security Council to Veto Malicious Transactions ENS DAO has approved a new eight-member Security Council with a two-year term and a 5-of-8 multisig. The council may cancel transactions deemed malicious during the two-day timelock after a governance proposal passes, but cannot move treasury funds, submit or amend proposals, or override DAO decisions. Its authority will automatically expire unless extended by another DAO vote. — link

text: Polymarket Refers Nearly 100 Suspicious Wallets to Law Enforcement Polymarket said it has strengthened its insider-trading surveillance and referred nearly 100 suspicious wallets to law enforcement. According to Bloomberg's analysis of Polysights data, about $200 million in trades on the platform during the first half of 2026 were flagged for characteristics associated with potential insider activity, largely involving geopolitical markets tied to Iran and Venezuela. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Trump Agrees to Ethics Provision, Clearing Key Hurdle for CLARITY Act According to The Block, US President Donald Trump has agreed to an ethics provision in the CLARITY Act, removing the final major hurdle to advancing the crypto market structure bill toward a Senate vote. The provision would restrict presidents, vice presidents, members of Congress and other federal officials from profiting from digital assets while in office. The bill text is expected to be released in the coming days, with the Senate facing a deadline in the first week of August. If passed, the bill will return to the House before being sent to Trump for signature. — link
